# Research - 2026-06-22

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 1-day delta on 2026-06-21. Three NEW items, all from opencode's
> 06-21 hardening batch. #33259 exposes `webfetch.ts:110` unbounded
> `response.text()` + missing Content-Length guard. #33266 maps to
> `streamingOrchestrator.ts:141-150` losing partial text + usage when
> streamComplete throws mid-flight (zero try/catch). #33260 N/A:
> `read.ts:373-394` already separates ENOENT/EACCES/binary. kilocode
> v7.3.51/52 was recall-tail (we have no recall tool). aider 30-day
> silence; claude-code 100% changelog confirms #772.

## Executive Summary

- **opencode #33259 -> Item #1 (~3h)**: `webfetch.ts:110` does
  `response.text()` with no Content-Length pre-check and no byte cap
  during read. Misbehaving site OOMs the agent. opencode added
  `collectBoundedResponseBody` (header-size fail-fast, capped Buffer
  grow, `convert()` wrapped in Effect.try).
- **opencode #33266 -> Item #2 (~4h)**: `streamingOrchestrator.ts`
  has zero error handling around the for-await loop. If the stream
  rejects, session save + cost record at lines 153-171 never run;
  user loses partial text AND usage. opencode added a `failAssistant`
  path that flushes, emits Step.Failed, settles state.
- **Item #3 (~2h)**: docs-only. `docs/PROVIDERS.md` lacks a
  streaming-error contract. Pre-emptive doc before #813 multi-model
  routing lands more providers that could silently swallow errors.
- **Carryover BLOCKER**: #818, #817 unlabeled `auto-implement` after
  1 day. #812, #810, #804 unlabeled after 4 days. ZERO open
  `auto-implement` issues across 30 enhancement tickets. 4th brief
  in a row flagging this.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Bound webfetch response body + wrap HTML converter (Category: tool, ~3h)

- **What**: In `webfetch.ts:108-115`, replace `response.text()`
  with a `collectBoundedResponseBody(response, MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES)`
  helper that (a) reads `content-length`, rejects when it exceeds
  the cap, (b) streams `response.body` chunk-by-chunk summing
  `byteLength` and aborting on overrun, (c) decodes to text only
  after the bounded buffer is assembled. Wrap the `htmlToText()`
  call at line 114 in try/catch so a regex backtracking throw
  becomes a ToolResult error. Default cap 5 MB. Test streams a
  body larger than cap; assert `success: false`.
- **Why**: A 100 MB HTML page buffers fully into V8 string memory
  and crashes the agent before truncateOutput. Trust-the-server
  network tools are textbook DoS-by-upstream vectors. Our
  `truncateOutput` at line 117 only runs AFTER the whole body is
  in memory -- too late.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33259 (commit `69f1ec22`, 2026-06-21,
  `+95 -28`, http-body.ts + webfetch.ts).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~3h).

#### 2. Settle streaming errors with partial save (Category: core, ~4h)

- **What**: Wrap the for-await loop in
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts:141-150` in try/catch. In catch: if
  `sessionManager` is set, call `addMessage('assistant', fullText +
  '\n\n[stream interrupted: ' + err.message + ']', { output:
  finalUsage?.completion_tokens })`, call
  `getCostTracker().recordUsage(...)` when usage was observed,
  re-throw. Mirror around `provider.complete()` at
  `agenticChat.ts:534`. Test: mock provider yields one chunk then
  throws; assert session has the partial.
- **Why**: Code silently drops partial assistant text + usage on
  any stream interruption (rate limit, socket close, malformed
  SSE). User retries and pays twice; `~/.alexi/sessions/` gets a
  gap. opencode's `publish-llm-event.ts` rewrite encodes the same
  rule: provider errors MUST transition through a terminal state.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33266 (commit `49593c1e`, 2026-06-21,
  `+69 -17`, publish-llm-event.ts + to-llm-message.ts).
- **Category**: core. **Size**: small (~4h).

#### 3. Document streaming-error contract (Category: config, ~2h)

- **What**: Add "Streaming error semantics" to `docs/PROVIDERS.md`
  after the capability matrix. Spell out what every
  `streamComplete` MUST honor: (a) network errors surface as
  rejected promises on the generator, (b) MAY yield a final
  `{ usage }` chunk before throwing, (c) MUST NOT swallow errors
  and return early. Link from Item #2. Note a shared test helper
  at `src/providers/__tests__/streaming-contract.ts`.
- **Why**: Item #2 is wasted if a future provider silently catches
  its own errors and ends cleanly -- orchestrator then writes a
  "success" entry with truncated text. Lock the contract before
  more providers land via #813 multi-model.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#33266 (commit `49593c1e`, 2026-06-21).
- **Category**: config. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Unblock label pipeline FIRST**: 30 open enhancement tickets,
   zero `auto-implement`. Run `gh issue edit 818 817 812 810 804
   --add-label auto-implement` today and audit
   `agent2-planning.yml` post-run.
2. **Order**: #1 (3h, DoS) -> #2 (4h, data integrity) -> #3 (2h,
   doc lock-in). #1 and #2 share test scaffolding (mock fetch
   throws mid-stream); co-implementation halves setup.
3. **Demote claude-code polling**: 5/9 commits this week are
   `CHANGELOG.md`. Land #772 weekly demotion to free `gh api`
   quota for opencode + kilocode.
